nodes.label: Express Data Access Boundary
nodes.rationale: The browser never receives the service-role key or queries application tables directly; authenticated application data access is routed through Express.

nodes.label: Database-Authoritative Roles
nodes.rationale: Express trusts the role stored in public.users rather than browser-supplied role metadata, and derives booking ownership from the authenticated user.

nodes.label: Service-Role Key Isolation
nodes.rationale: The service-role key is restricted to guarded server-side maintenance commands and must not be exposed through frontend variables.

nodes.label: Migration 002 Supabase Auth and Owner Applications
nodes.norm_label: migration 002 supabase auth and owner applications

nodes.label: Transactional Owner Approval
nodes.rationale: Approval locks and updates a pending application in one transaction while promoting the customer, preventing a completed application from being reviewed twice.

nodes.label: Web Data and Authorization Boundary
nodes.rationale: The browser uses Supabase only for authentication sessions, while all application-table operations pass through Express with the current access token.
